task: I’m helping someone who’s upset because a correction is taking longer than they expected, and I need to send a brief update before the next step is finalized. Can you help me draft a simple message that explains the timing and what happens next?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, steady version you can send:

> I understand this is frustrating. I’ve confirmed the issue and I’m still verifying the final details so we can make the correct adjustment. I’m not able to finalize the next step yet, but I will send you another update by [day/time] with the confirmed status and what happens next.

If you want, I can also make it:
- firmer
- more empathetic
- shorter for text/Slack
- tailored to a payroll cutoff situation

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine but a little too generic. I need it to sound more concrete — like the correction is in process, it’s going to take two pay cycles or whatever the actual timing is, and the person will get the next update when the review is done. Short. No fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a shorter, more concrete version:

> The correction is in process and will take [two pay cycles / X days] to complete. The review is still underway, and I’ll send the next update as soon as it’s finished with the confirmed next step.

If you want, I can make it even tighter for text or Slack.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, concrete, and not fluffy.
